high_value_elements: Correctly identifies PBM target: cytochrome C oxidase in mitochondria; downstream mediators: ATP, nitric oxide, reactive oxygen species; wavelength range 635-904 nm
Accurately explains locoregional mechanism: sodium channel blockade preventing action potential propagation along peripheral nerve fibers

Correctly identifies electroacupuncture analgesia: low frequency (2 Hz) stimulates release of enkephalins and beta-endorphins; high frequency (100 Hz) stimulates dynorphin release at spinal cord level
Describes epidural analgesia as neuraxial blockade of afferent nociceptive signals at the spinal level; correctly identifies L7-S1 as the standard lumbosacral landmark in dogs
Associates each technique with an anatomically and mechanistically appropriate primary clinical indication
major_concerns: Confuses PBM photobiomodulation with thermal/surgical laser effects or cannot state the mitochondrial target
Cannot explain how any of the four technique categories produces analgesia mechanistically
Cannot distinguish locoregional from systemic analgesia in mechanism or clinical application

high_value_elements: Selects anatomically appropriate nerve branches for TECA-BO: inferior alveolar, auriculotemporal, and great auricular nerves with rationale for each relative to surgical field
Calculates maximum safe local anesthetic dose for a 34 kg dog; recognizes cats have a substantially lower IV lidocaine toxicity threshold (~47 mg/kg) compared to dogs (~80 mg/kg)
Integrates postoperative PBM or acupuncture into a coherent multimodal plan with mechanistic rationale
Applies laser parameter adjustments for a large, dark-coated, obese dog: increased fluence for dark coat absorption, depth penetration adjustment for BCS 8/9
Describes loss of resistance technique as the standard confirmation method for epidural needle placement
major_concerns: Selects a locoregional technique anatomically inappropriate for the TECA-BO surgical field
Cannot describe or calculate maximum safe local anesthetic dose; does not recognize species difference in LA toxicity threshold
Proposes laser as a standalone analgesic replacing pharmacological management

high_value_elements: Prioritizes full contraindication screening before any PBM session: active neoplasia, cardiac arrhythmia or pacemaker, open growth plates, pregnancy, active hemorrhage, eyes unprotected, deep active infection
Identifies Doberman breed risk for dilated cardiomyopathy; recommends cardiac screening before any modality using electrical current near the thorax
Sequences technique delivery logically within a perioperative timeline
Correctly identifies when a technique is outside personal scope and describes a safe referral pathway
major_concerns: Fails to identify active neoplasia as an absolute PBM contraindication
Does not screen for cardiac disease in a Doberman before electroacupuncture
Cannot identify LAST as a serious complication or describe its clinical signs

high_value_elements: Provides mechanism-based rationale for nerve branch selection at TECA-BO referencing the sensory territory of each branch
Explains why electroacupuncture at 2-100 Hz range targets different endogenous opioid peptides at spinal versus supraspinal levels
Explains why dark coat requires higher fluence and how the clinician adjusts parameters to maintain target tissue dose
Justifies choice of ultrasound guidance versus nerve stimulator versus combined approach for specific block type
major_concerns: Makes technique selections without supporting mechanistic or anatomical rationale
Cannot explain why a chosen nerve block is anatomically appropriate for the surgical site

high_value_elements: Acknowledges scope-of-competence limits for techniques requiring additional hands-on credentialing
States full PBM contraindication list: neoplasia, active hemorrhage, pregnancy, unprotected eyes, open growth plates, deep active infection, active cardiac arrhythmia, pacemaker
Describes appropriate credentialing pathway before independently performing new locoregional or neuraxial techniques
major_concerns: Cannot name a single contraindication to Class IV laser therapy
Claims readiness for techniques clearly requiring supervised training without acknowledging skill gap

high_value_elements: Integrates multiple techniques synergistically with mechanistic rationale for each combination and dose-sparing benefit
Correctly applies continuous wave versus superpulsed laser distinction with clinical implications for thermal safety and dose delivery
Can discuss force plate gait analysis as an objective outcome measure to evaluate response to acupuncture or other techniques
Correctly identifies loss of resistance technique for epidural confirmation and the significance of the L7-S1 landmark
major_concerns: Cannot discuss any interaction or synergy between techniques
Names techniques without understanding clinical decision points or delivery parameters
